title: [Bug]: Scheduled Tasks ignore Instance Timezone and run on UTC
author: ahturkoz
body:
### Error Message and Logs

Instance Timezone is set to Europe/Istanbul in Settings, and the host system confirms the correct timezone via `timedatectl` (Europe/Istanbul, +03, NTP synced). However, Scheduled Tasks continue to execute based on UTC, ignoring the Instance Timezone setting entirely. This causes all scheduled tasks/reports to run 3 hours later than intended.

Diagnostic output:
- `docker exec -it coolify date` → correct Istanbul time (TRT)
- `docker exec -it coolify env | grep -i tz` → TZ=TRT-3
- Instance Timezone in UI → Europe/Istanbul

Host and container clocks are both correct. Only the Scheduled Task execution logic appears to ignore the Instance Timezone and run on UTC instead.

Related to #5221.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to Settings and set Instance Timezone to Europe/Istanbul
2. Create a Scheduled Task with a cron expression intended for a specific time in Istanbul timezone (e.g. 23:59)
3. Observe that the task actually executes 3 hours later, based on UTC, not Istanbul time

### Example Repository URL

### Coolify Version

4.1.2

title: [Bug]: Fresh Supabase Marketplace deployment fails because Edge Runtime cannot determine main entrypoint
author: volkanturkeli
body: ### Error Message and Logs A fresh Supabase deployment from the Marketplace resulted in the `supabase-edge-functions` container continuously restarting. The container logs show: ```text worker boot error: failed to bootstrap runtime failed to determine entrypoint Error: main worker boot error ``` During investigation I found that the expected entrypoint `functions/main/index.ts` was not a file. Replacing it with a valid `index.ts` file immediately resolved the issue and the service became healthy. I am not sure whether this is caused by the Marketplace template, Docker bind mounts or Coolify's deployment process, but I was able to reproduce it on a completely fresh installation. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Install Ubuntu 24.04. 2. Install Coolify 4.1.2. 3. Deploy Supabase from the Marketplace. 4. Wait for deployment. 5. Observe that `supabase-edge-functions` enters a restart loop with "failed to determine entrypoint". ### Example Repository URL _No response_ ### Coolify Version 4.1.2 ### Are you using Coolify Cloud? No (self-hosted) ### Operating System and Version (self-hosted) 24.04.4 LTS (Noble Numbat) ### Additional Information Environment - Ubuntu 24.04 LTS - Docker 29.6.1 - Coolify 4.1.2 This was a completely fresh installation. During investigation I observed: Initially: functions/main/index.ts -> directory After removing it and creating an actual file: functions/main/index.ts -> file The Edge Runtime started immediately after this change. Current container layout: /home/deno/functions/main/index.ts (file) /home/deno/functions/hello/index.ts (directory) The service is healthy after replacing only `main/index.ts`.

title: [Bug]: Random build failures on 4.1.2
author: vitusan
body:
### Error Message and Logs

After updating to this latest coolify version, i can see a perceptible degrade in performance in both the UI as the container build process.

Pages take longer to load and deployments fail randomly at random steps both using nixpack and/or railpack.

And they fail on weird steps of the process, not related to the app itself (builds are happening normally), but when coolify/nixpax/railpack tries to touch folders or files, even the docker images (like when exporting layers), sometimes, i don't know why, it fails to advance and crashes.

It's not a VPS limitation. 16 gb RAM and 4 cores Ryzen epic, it was doing the job before v4.1.X reliably.

title: [Bug]: Can not select the Server to create a new App on
author: niklaslrksallguth
body: ### Error Message and Logs I am running Coolify on my VPS A. I have added VPS B as a server to coolify. I am able to create resources on VPS B via the Coolify API. However, when creating a resource via the Web UI, it will always use the localhost server and i have no way of selecting any other server. The server configuration looks fine and is also validated. All resources deployed via API on B work fine too. I can not find any reason, why i can't select which server to use when creating a resource. It's also not possible for me to clone a resource to B from Resource Operations. So for some reason, the Web UI acts like VPS B does not exist, but properly displays it in the server overview and also for the resources created via API. ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Add a server to coolify 2. Try to create a new Resource on the external server ### Example Repository URL _No response_ ### Coolify Version v4.1.2 ### Are you using Coolify Cloud? No (self-hosted) ### Operating System and Version (self-hosted) _No response_ ### Additional Information

title: [Bug]: S3 Storage validates successfully but cannot be selected for Instance Backups (v4.1.2)
author: arafat-alim-office
body: ### Error Message and Logs # Description I'm running a **self-hosted Coolify v4.1.2** on a **Hostinger VPS**. I successfully configured an **AWS S3 Storage**. The connection validates successfully, the storage is marked as **Usable**, and the configuration is saved without any errors. However, when I navigate to **Settings → Backup**, there is no way to select the configured S3 storage for instance backups. The only available backup destination is Local Storage. --- # Steps to reproduce 1. Install Coolify v4.1.2 on a VPS. 2. Navigate to **S3 Storages**. 3. Create a new AWS S3 storage. 4. Enter: * Endpoint * Region * Bucket * Access Key * Secret Key 5. Click **Validate Connection**. 6. Validation succeeds. 7. Save the storage. 8. Navigate to **Settings → Backup**. --- # Expected behavior I expected to be able to: * Enable S3 backups * Select the previously created S3 Storage from a dropdown or similar selector * Save the backup configuration --- # Actual behavior The backup page only shows: * Backup Enabled * S3 Enabled (checkbox) * Disable Local Backup There is **no dropdown or option to choose the configured S3 Storage**. The storage is never associated with the backup configuration. The S3 Storage page also displays: > No backup schedules are using this storage. --- # Environment * Coolify Version: **v4.1.2** * Deployment: **Self Hosted** * VPS Provider: **Hostinger** * Operating System: Ubuntu 24.04 * Docker Installation: Official Coolify installation script * Storage Provider: AWS S3 * Region: ap-south-1 --- # AWS S3 configuration Endpoint ``` https://s3.ap-south-1.amazonaws.com ``` Region ``` ap-south-1 ``` Bucket ``` ``` Connection validation returns: ``` Connection is working. Tested with "ListObjectsV2" action. ``` Saving the storage returns: ``` Success Storage settings updated and connection verified. ``` --- # Things I've already tried * Hard refresh * Different browser * Incognito mode * Restarted Coolify * Rebooted the VPS * Recreated the S3 Storage * Revalidated the connection * Confirmed storage is marked as **Usable** * Confirmed bucket permissions are correct (ListObjectsV2 succeeds) None of these resolved the issue. --- # Browser Console The only console message I observed is: ``` WebSocket connection to 'wss://coolify.example.com/app/...' failed: WebSocket is closed before the connection is established. ``` I'm not sure if this is related, but there are no visible API errors when opening the Backup page. --- # Additional information The S3 Storage is successfully saved and recognized by Coolify. However, there is currently no UI element that allows selecting this storage for instance backups. Because of this, backups can only be stored locally. I'm unsure whether this is: * a UI regression, * a backend issue, * or an intended behavior that is currently undocumented. Could someone please confirm whether this is a bug or if an additional configuration step is required? --- # Screenshots ### S3 Storage (Successfully validated) --- ### Backup Settings (No S3 storage selector available) --- Demonstrating the issue When I click the S3 Enabled checkbox, it immediately becomes unchecked again. The Backup Enabled checkbox works correctly, but S3 Enabled cannot be enabled. ### Coolify Version --- ## VPS Terminall Run: ```bash docker logs coolify --tail=300 ``` ```bash Welcome to Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (GNU/Linux 6.8.0-134-generic x86_64) * Documentation: https://help.ubuntu.com * Management: https://landscape.canonical.com * Support: https://ubuntu.com/pro System information as of Fri Jul 10 08:18:03 UTC 2026 System load: 0.44 Processes: 497 Usage of /: 11.0% of 386.42GB Users logged in: 0 Memory usage: 18% IPv4 address for eth0: 187.127.167.57 Swap usage: 0% IPv6 address for eth0: 2a02:4780:63:b75b::1 * Strictly confined Kubernetes makes edge and IoT secure. title: [Enhancement]: Improve dashboard with operational KPIs and healthcheck visibility
author: hector-rcc
body:
### Request Type

Improvement

### Description

The current Coolify dashboard is useful for onboarding, but once you have servers and resources deployed it does not provide a quick operational overview of what is running, what recently deployed, and what may need attention.

I would like to propose improving the dashboard with:

1. **KPI cards** for servers, projects, applications, services, and databases (total / active / inactive)
2. **Latest Deployments** panel with status and quick links to deployment history
3. **Connected Servers** panel with reachability status
4. **Down without healthcheck** section listing stopped resources that have no healthcheck configured, with links to enable monitoring

This helps teams notice silent failures earlier, especially for resources deployed without healthchecks.

### Motivation

- The dashboard today focuses on onboarding CTAs more than day-2 operations
- Stopped resources without healthchecks are easy to miss until something breaks downstream
- A single overview reduces context switching between Projects, Servers, and individual resource pages

### Proposed implementation

Community PR in progress from @hector-rcc (will target `next`).

### Preview

![Dashboard overview with KPIs, deployments, servers, and healthcheck alerts](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hector-rcc/coolify/feat/dashboard/.github/pr-previews/dashboard-overview.png)

### Additional context

Happy to adjust scope based on maintainer feedback (e.g. whether healthcheck alerts should be a separate page vs dashboard section).

title: [Bug]: PR preview deploys ignore [skip ci]/[skip cd] in the head commit — only the PR title is checked
author: ton77v
body:
### Error Message and Logs

Not an error — an incomplete behavior of the `[skip ci]` / `[skip cd]` feature shipped in v4.1.0 (#9861).

Pushing a commit whose message contains `[skip ci]` to an **open PR** still triggers a new **preview** deployment. The skip markers are honored for `push` webhooks, but **not** for GitHub PR (`synchronize`) events.

Root cause (v4.x source, confirmed against v4.1.2): `ProcessGithubPullRequestWebhook` calls `shouldSkipDeployAny([$pullRequestTitle])` — it passes only the **PR title**, never the head-commit message. GitHub's `pull_request` webhook payload carries no commit messages, so on `synchronize` there is no commit text to match against; the per-commit path (`DetectsSkipDeployCommits`) only runs for `push` webhooks. Net effect: for PR previews, `[skip ci]` works **only** when placed in the PR *title* (a whole-PR kill switch) — never per-commit.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Connect a GitHub repo via the GitHub App; enable PR preview deployments for an application.
2. Open a PR → a preview deploys (expected).
3. Push a follow-up commit to the PR branch with `[skip ci]` in its message (e.g. `docs: fix typo [skip ci]`).
4. A new preview deployment is still triggered for that commit. Expected (parity with `push` webhooks): the deploy is skipped.

### Example Repository URL

N/A — source-level; reproducible on any GitHub-App-connected repo with previews enabled.

### Coolify Version

v4.1.2

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

Yes (Coolify Cloud)

### Operating System and Version (self-hosted)

N/A (Coolify Cloud) — behavior is host-independent (webhook payload handling).

### Additional Information

**Origin.** The `[skip ci]` / `[skip cd]` feature (#9861, v4.1.0) covered `push` webhooks and the PR *title*, but not the per-commit case for PR previews. History: original report #4357 → discussion #4358, closed as resolved by #9861 — the preview per-commit case survived that closure.

**Proposed minimal fix.** On `synchronize`, fetch the head commit's message via one GitHub API call (the GitHub App token is already available) and pass it alongside the title — `shouldSkipDeployAny([$pullRequestTitle, $headCommitMessage])` — giving PR previews parity with the `push`-webhook skip. Could sit behind the existing skip-deploy setting.

**Why it matters.** This is not monorepo-specific and predates any monorepo layout — it bites any instance with a busy CI pipeline or heavy preview images, and large/complex infra simply amplifies it. A `[skip ci]` on a docs- or generated-file-only commit still forces a full preview rebuild; when several such commits land on one PR they queue serially and exhaust build capacity, delaying the preview that actually matters. The two available workarounds are both unsatisfying: the PR-*title* lever is all-or-nothing (it kills *every* preview on the PR, not the one commit), and disabling auto-deploy forfeits Coolify-managed preview lifecycle/cleanup. Per-commit skip parity for previews is the clean fix.

title: [Bug]: Instance database (coolify-db) scheduled backup silently never runs — ScheduledJobManager pagination skips id 0
author: alexandro737
body:
## Description

The scheduled backup of Coolify's own instance database (`coolify-db`) is silently never dispatched. The `ScheduledDatabaseBackup` row for the instance database is created with the sentinel primary key `id = 0`:

- `app/Livewire/SettingsBackup.php` — `ScheduledDatabaseBackup::create(['id' => 0, ...])`
- `app/Console/Commands/Init.php` (`restoreCoolifyDbBackup()`) — same

But `app/Jobs/ScheduledJobManager.php` fetches due backups with keyset pagination that starts **above** 0:

```php
$lastBackupId = 0;
// ...
private function scheduledBackupQuery(int $lastBackupId): Builder
{
    return ScheduledDatabaseBackup::with(['database', 'team.subscription'])
        ->where('enabled', true)
        ->where('id', '>', $lastBackupId)   // <-- id 0 can never match
        ->orderBy('id')
        ->limit(self::CHUNK_SIZE);
}
```

The first page selects `id > 0`, so the row with `id = 0` — the only row in the system with that id, and the one protecting Coolify's own configuration — is excluded from every chunk, forever. There is no error, no failed execution, no log entry: executions simply stop.

`dueScheduledTasks` / `scheduledTaskQuery` use the same pattern, but scheduled tasks are never seeded with id 0, so only the instance backup is affected.

## Impact

Every Coolify instance running a version with `ScheduledJobManager` has a dead instance-DB backup, while the UI (Settings → Backup) still shows it as enabled and shows the old (pre-upgrade) executions. Users who rely on that backup to protect their app/env/deployment configuration have silently lost it.

## Observed timeline (our instance)

- Instance created 2026-03, instance backup ran daily at 02:00 without issues.
- Coolify auto-updated to 4.1.2 in mid-June.
- Last successful execution: Jun 17, 02:00. Zero executions (success or failure) after that.
- Verified the same code is present on `main` as of 2026-07-08.

## Suggested fix

Any of:

1. Initialize the cursor below zero: `$lastBackupId = -1;` (and same for tasks, defensively), or
2. Use `>=` with an explicit "already processed" guard, or
3. Stop seeding the instance backup with `id = 0` and identify it another way (e.g. a flag column or by `database_id = 0`), migrating existing rows.

## Workaround for affected users

Move the row off id 0 (the Settings UI finds it via the `database` relationship, not by id, so this is safe):

```sql
BEGIN;
DELETE FROM scheduled_database_backup_executions WHERE scheduled_database_backup_id = 0;
UPDATE scheduled_database_backups
  SET id = nextval(pg_get_serial_sequence('scheduled_database_backups','id'))
  WHERE id = 0;
COMMIT;
```

Verified working: the scheduler picks the row up on the next sweep and backups (including S3 upload) succeed again.

## Version

- Affected: 4.1.2 (observed), `main` @ 2026-07-08 (code inspection)

title: Add instance-level default and per-application toggle to control search engine indexing (X-Robots-Tag)
author: albertorizzi
body:
### Error Message and Logs

Coolify currently does not provide any mechanism to control whether deployed applications 
are indexed by search engines. This means all applications on custom subdomains are 
publicly indexable by default, including staging environments, work-in-progress projects, 
and internal tools.

The proposed feature has two levels:

1. Instance-level default (Settings)
   A global toggle in Coolify's Settings page to define the default indexing behavior 
   for all applications:
   - "Allow indexing" (current behavior, default for backward compatibility)
   - "Block indexing" (injects X-Robots-Tag: noindex via Traefik middleware on all apps)

2. Per-application override (Application > Configuration)
   A toggle on each application's configuration page that overrides the instance default, 
   allowing specific apps to deviate from the global setting.
   Example: instance default is "Block indexing", but the production app at example.com 
   can be individually set to "Allow indexing".

Implementation-wise, this could be achieved by having Coolify automatically add or remove 
a Traefik middleware that injects the X-Robots-Tag: noindex HTTP response header based 
on the configured setting.

### Steps to Reproduce

A typical self-hosted setup includes multiple applications on subdomains of the same 
root domain (e.g. app.node1.example.com, staging.node1.example.com, internal.node1.example.com).

Only one or two of these are actual production apps that should be indexed. The rest 
should be invisible to search engines. Currently there is no way to enforce this without 
manually configuring Traefik middlewares for each application.

Platforms like Vercel and Cloudflare Pages handle this automatically:
- Vercel: injects X-Robots-Tag: noindex on all non-production deployments
- Cloudflare Pages: injects X-Robots-Tag: noindex on all preview deployments

Coolify, as a self-hosted PaaS alternative, should offer at minimum equivalent control.

title: Deployments fail with exit code 255 mid-build: mux refresh (ssh -O exit) kills in-flight sessions after SSH_MUX_MAX_AGE
author: Omerk15
body:
## Description

On long-running deployments (docker-compose buildpack, builds > a few minutes), deployments sporadically fail with:

```
Deployment failed: Command execution failed (exit code 255): docker exec <helper> bash -c '... docker compose ... build'
Error type: App\Exceptions\DeploymentException
Location: /var/www/html/app/Traits/ExecuteRemoteCommand.php:242
```

dockerd log at the same second shows `/moby.buildkit.v1.Control/Solve error="rpc error: code = Canceled desc = context canceled"`. No OOM, no disk pressure, server load is low.

## Root cause

All server commands share one multiplexed SSH connection (ControlMaster). `SshMultiplexingHelper::ensureMultiplexedConnection()` treats the connection as non-reusable once it is older than `SSH_MUX_MAX_AGE` (default 1800 s) and calls `refreshMultiplexedConnection()`, which runs `removeMuxFile()` → `ssh -O exit`. **`-O exit` terminates the master including all active channels** — so any other Coolify job (health poll, scheduled task) that touches the connection while a deployment command is streaming kills that deployment's build stream. The deployment fails with exit 255; buildkit reports "context canceled".

## Evidence (Coolify 4.1.2, Ubuntu 24.04, localhost server)

Failed deployments correlate to the second with a *new* master login in the host's auth log (the refresh):

| Deployment failed_at | New master login (`journalctl -u ssh`, source = Coolify container IP) |
|---|---|
| 2026-07-01 21:56:26 | 21:56:25 |
| 2026-07-02 16:24:21 | 16:24:20 |
| 2026-07-02 21:24:21 (log stream cut) | 21:24 refresh slot |

Login cadence: ~45 new master connections/day ≈ exactly every 30 min (= default `SSH_MUX_MAX_AGE`). Failure probability ≈ build duration / 30 min — cold builds (~25 min) failed ~80 % of the time for us.

## Suggested fix

In `refreshMultiplexedConnection()`, use `ssh -O stop` (stop accepting new sessions, keep existing ones alive) instead of `-O exit`, then establish a fresh master on a new/rotated ControlPath — or skip the age-based refresh while a deployment job holds the connection.

## Workaround

`SSH_MUX_MAX_AGE=86400` in the instance `.env` reduces the kill window from 48×/day to 1×/day. The health check still recycles genuinely broken connections.

title: [Bug]: Service environment variable values become empty strings in DB after image redeploy (docker-mailserver stack)
author: itsbrody
body:
### Error Message and Logs


**Resource type:** Service (Docker Compose stack), specifically a docker-mailserver stack

## Description

After triggering a redeploy (pull new image + restart) of a Service-type resource, all environment variable values for that service were empty strings in the Coolify database — not missing, not undecryptable, but stored as an empty string that decrypts successfully.

Verified via:
`docker exec -it coolify php artisan tinker --execute="var_dump(decrypt('<stored_value>'));"`

Result: `string(0) ""` — no decryption exception, just an empty value.

This caused the container to fail to start, since `docker-mailserver`'s `start-mailserver.sh` requires `hostname`/`domainname` to be set (as logfile shows by):

ERROR start-mailserver.sh: Setting hostname/domainname is required
ERROR start-mailserver.sh: Shutting down

### Steps to Reproduce

Unknown — we were not able to reproduce this on demand. Reporting based on a single observed incident. Happy to provide more DB query output or logs if that helps triage.

see more details in "Additional Information"

### Example Repository URL

_No response_

### Coolify Version

4.1.2

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

No (self-hosted)

### Operating System and Version (self-hosted)

Ubuntu 24.04

### Additional Information

## What we ruled out

- Not a UI display bug — checked the database directly, values are genuinely empty strings.
- Not a decryption/APP_KEY mismatch — decrypt() succeeds cleanly, just returns an empty string.
- Not a general instance-wide issue — two other Service-stack resources (n8n, Nextcloud, both also Compose-based, both also redeployed around the same time) were unaffected; their environment_variables rows remained populated.
- Not a "which server" difference — the affected service and the two unaffected services do not share the same host, so it is not host-specific.

## Possible lead (unconfirmed)

In the affected service's docker-compose.yml, one variable interpolation is lowercase (`${domainname}`) while all others in the same file are uppercase (`${LETSENCRYPT_DOMAIN}`, `${LETSENCRYPT_EMAIL}`, `${POSTMASTER_ADDRESS}`). In the two unaffected services' compose files, all variable interpolations are uppercase without exception. We do not know if this is related — flagging it as an observed difference, not a confirmed cause.

## Recovery

Restored the correct values from a Restic backup taken before the incident (a snapshot of `/data/coolify`), re-entered them via the UI's Developer View, and confirmed via direct DB query that values persisted correctly through a subsequent redeploy.

title: [Bug]: COOLIFY_URL / COOLIFY_FQDN mangled when a compose service has multiple comma-separated domains (set_coolify_variables parses the list as one URL)
author: evanmroche
body:
### Error Message and Logs

When a Docker Compose application has **multiple comma-separated domains on one service** (e.g. an edge/reverse-proxy service fronting several hosts), the `COOLIFY_URL` / `COOLIFY_FQDN` variables that `set_coolify_variables()` prepends to the `docker compose up` command line are mangled:

```
COOLIFY_URL=https://35.admin.example.com,https//35.gate.example.com,https://35.resident.example.com,https://35.api.example.com,https://35.example.com
COOLIFY_FQDN=35.admin.example.com,https
```

Note the second URL lost the colon in its scheme (`https//`), and `COOLIFY_FQDN` is truncated to the first host plus a stray `https`.

The values stored in the DB (`applications.docker_compose_domains`, `application_previews.fqdn`) are correct — the corruption happens only in `set_coolify_variables()`.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Docker Compose application and assign multiple domains to a single service, e.g. `https://a.example.com,https://b.example.com`.
2. Deploy (production or PR preview — both paths hit the same code).
3. Inspect the deployment log's `docker compose up` command: `COOLIFY_URL` / `COOLIFY_FQDN` are malformed as above.

### Root cause

`app/Jobs/ApplicationDeploymentJob.php`, `set_coolify_variables()` (present in v4.1.2 and current `main`, line ~2240):

```php
if (isset($fqdn)) {
    $url = Url::fromString($fqdn);
    $fqdn = $url->getHost();
    $url = $url->withHost($fqdn)->withPort(null)->__toString();
```

The whole comma-separated list is passed to `Url::fromString()` as if it were a single URL. The parser then treats the **second** scheme's `:` as a host:port separator, so:

- `getHost()` returns `a.example.com,https` (→ broken `COOLIFY_FQDN`)
- re-serializing drops that colon, producing `https://a.example.com,https//b.example.com` (→ broken `COOLIFY_URL`)

By contrast, the label/env generation in `bootstrap/helpers/parsers.php` handles this correctly by mapping over each fqdn individually before joining with commas.

### Suggested fix

Split on commas and parse each entry, mirroring `parsers.php`:

```php
if (isset($fqdn)) {
    $parsed = str($fqdn)->explode(',')->map(fn ($entry) => Url::fromString(trim($entry)));
    $fqdn = $parsed->map(fn ($entry) => $entry->getHost())->implode(',');
    $url = $parsed->map(fn ($entry) => $entry->withPort(null)->__toString())->implode(',');
    ...
```

### Example Repository URL

_No response_

### Coolify Version

v4.1.2

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

No (self-hosted)

### Operating System and Version (self-hosted)

Ubuntu 24.04

### Additional Information

The bug is mostly cosmetic unless the compose file or app consumes `COOLIFY_URL`/`COOLIFY_FQDN`, in which case apps receive a malformed URL list. Confirmed the same code is still present on `main` as of 2026-07-01.

title: [Bug]: Hermes Agent template does not support Dashboard/Desktop remote backend correctly
author: finnicius
body:
**PR was created to address this issue:** https://github.com/coollabsio/coolify/pull/10821

## Error Message and Logs

The current Hermes Agent one-click service template has two related problems:

1. It does not expose/configure the Hermes Dashboard surface, so a Coolify deployment can be usable through the separate WebUI while still being incomplete as a remote backend for Hermes Desktop. Hermes Desktop connects to the Dashboard endpoint, not the separate WebUI surface.

2. It pins an older Hermes Agent image and mounts the `hermes-agent-src` volume over `/opt/hermes` in the main agent container:

```yaml
image: 'nousresearch/hermes-agent@sha256:2f1f2f1725e5dc9a61cf6a2dea5aca52a776ec4d022cb29679e6aa3ff303e77a' # v2026.5.29
volumes:
  - hermes-home:/home/hermes/.hermes
  - hermes-agent-src:/opt/hermes
```

That volume masks the install tree baked into newer `nousresearch/hermes-agent` images. Updating/pulling a newer image can therefore still leave the container running stale code from the persistent volume.

This affects remote Hermes Desktop/Dashboard setups. Newer Hermes Agent images expose `/api/status` as a public auth-shape probe, but a deployment using the stale `/opt/hermes` volume can keep returning `401 unauthenticated` / `no_cookie` on `/api/status`, preventing Hermes Desktop from recognizing the remote gateway.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy the current `hermes-agent-with-webui` template.
2. Try to use the deployment as a remote backend from Hermes Desktop.
3. Update the Hermes Agent image to a newer tag/digest.
4. Redeploy the service while keeping the existing `hermes-agent-src` named volume.
5. Inspect the running agent container or request the dashboard status endpoint.

Expected behavior:

- The template exposes/configures the Hermes Dashboard endpoint needed by Hermes Desktop.
- The main agent container runs the `/opt/hermes` tree from the currently pulled image.

Actual behavior:

- The template exposes the WebUI but not the Dashboard surface expected by Hermes Desktop.
- `/opt/hermes` is overlaid by the persistent `hermes-agent-src` volume, so image updates may not take effect.

## Example Repository URL

https://github.com/coollabsio/coolify/blob/v4.x/templates/compose/hermes-agent-with-webui.yaml

## Coolify Version

Current `v4.x` / `next` service template in this repository.

## Are you using Coolify Cloud?

No (self-hosted)

## Operating System and Version (self-hosted)

Linux VPS / Docker Compose deployment through Coolify.

## Additional Information

A safer template structure is to keep persistent Hermes state under `HERMES_HOME` (`/home/hermes/.hermes`) and avoid mounting a named volume over `/opt/hermes` in the main agent container.

The WebUI still needs read-only access to Hermes Agent source today, so the fix can seed `hermes-agent-src` from the current image through a short init/source-copy service instead of mounting that volume over the main agent container's `/opt/hermes` path.

title: [Bug]: Deleting an S3 storage still linked to a backup leaves an orphaned reference and breaks the backup
author: Nirator78
body:
### Error Message and Logs

## Description

When an S3 storage is used as the destination for a backup (e.g. the Coolify
instance / database backup of the Coolify server itself) and that S3 storage is
then **deleted** from the Storages settings, Coolify does not clean up the
reference held by the backup configuration.

The backup keeps pointing to the now-deleted S3 storage. Coolify still "thinks"
the S3 destination exists, so the scheduled backup is blocked / fails instead of
falling back or reporting a clear error.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create an S3 storage in Coolify.
2. Configure a backup (e.g. the Coolify) to use that S3
   storage as its destination.
3. Delete the S3 storage from **Settings → Storages**.
4. Trigger the backup (or wait for the scheduled run).

## Expected behavior

One of the following:
- Prevent deletion of an S3 storage that is still referenced by a backup, **or**
- Warn the user and clean up / reset the affected backup destinations on
  deletion, **or**
- Gracefully fail the backup with a clear error pointing to the missing storage.

## Actual behavior

The S3 storage is deleted but the backup configuration keeps an orphaned
reference to it. The backup is blocked and fails because it tries to use a
storage that no longer exists.

### Coolify Version

v4.1.2

### Are you using Coolify Cloud?

No (self-hosted)

### Operating System and Version (self-hosted)

Ubuntu 24.04

### Additional Information

_No response_
